title: Hybrid Mixer + Cheap Routing Combo
research_question: Do the hybrid lower-stack mixer win and the cheap-routing redesign win stack, or are they mostly buying the same effect?

candidates.name: s1-full-cheap-routing-package
candidates.goal: Test the strongest justified architecture combo in the lab.
candidates.hypothesis: The hybrid lower-stack mixer win and the cheap-routing package attack different inefficiencies and should stack.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the best two live architecture families are complementary or redundant.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most justified combo tranche in the repo right now because both parent families already produced real wins.
candidates.description: apply shared skip gates, scalar resid_mix, and shared residual scales on top of the current hybrid winner

candidates.name: s2-shared-skip-only
candidates.goal: Test whether skip gating is the compatible subpiece.
candidates.hypothesis: Shared scalar skip gates may stack cleanly with the hybrid winner even if the rest of the routing package is too much.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether skip gating is the main complementary part of the routing redesign.
candidates.why_worthy: This cleanly isolates the strongest routing change without paying for the full package immediately.
candidates.description: add only shared scalar skip gates to the current hybrid winner

candidates.name: s3-scalar-resid-mix-only
candidates.goal: Test whether stream blending is the compatible subpiece.
candidates.hypothesis: The hybrid winner may want simpler residual-stream blending without also changing skip topology.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the main interaction is in stream mixing rather than skip routing.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest way to separate skip effects from stream-blending effects.
candidates.description: replace full per-channel resid_mix with a shared scalar on the current hybrid winner

candidates.name: s4-shared-residual-scales-only
candidates.goal: Test whether coarser branch scaling is the compatible part.
candidates.hypothesis: The hybrid winner may benefit from simpler branch scaling even if skip and stream routing stay unchanged.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether branch-scale simplification is the main compatible routing change.
candidates.why_worthy: This isolates the third routing component cleanly, which matters because the earlier routing tranche showed interaction effects.
candidates.description: use shared residual scales only on top of the current hybrid winner

candidates.name: s5-full-package-plus-head-cooldown
candidates.goal: Test whether the best combo branch wants one light support intervention near export.
candidates.hypothesis: If the routing combo is real, a head-focused final cooldown may help the compressed model close the remaining gap.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether combo branches need light schedule support rather than more architecture changes.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the only justified combo-on-combo run because head-focused cooldown was the only live schedule hint left.
candidates.description: apply the full cheap-routing package plus a head-focused final cooldown
